task_completion.description: Verify that the CT Cranium dataset is loaded into the software.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The InVesalius interface shows the loaded CT Cranium dataset with 2D slice views (axial, coronal, sagittal) visible.

task_completion.description: Verify that a custom threshold is set with a maximum of -100 HU to target air/sinuses.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The threshold panel in InVesalius shows a custom range where the maximum value is -100 or lower, and the 2D slices show a mask highlighting the empty air cavities/sinuses.

task_completion.description: Verify that a 3D surface of the air-filled cavities is generated.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 3D surface model representing the airways/sinuses is visible in the 3D viewer pane of InVesalius.

task_completion.description: Verify that the generated 3D surface is colored blue.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 3D surface model in the viewer pane is predominantly blue. The surface properties or color picker may also be visible showing blue as the selected color.

task_completion.description: Verify that the project is saved to the correct path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save As' dialog is visible showing the path '/home/ga/Documents/airway_study.inv3', or the main InVesalius window title bar displays 'airway_study.inv3'.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent used the InVesalius graphical user interface to complete the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent clicking through the InVesalius menus, threshold panels, and 3D surface generation buttons rather than running terminal scripts.

integrity.description: Ensure the 3D surface is genuinely generated within the software.
integrity.visual_evidence: The 3D surface appears inside the standard InVesalius 3D viewport with appropriate lighting and shading, matching the thresholded 2D mask slices.

integrity.description: Ensure the project file was saved via the application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The save action is performed via the InVesalius GUI (e.g., File -> Save Project As), rather than a terminal command creating a dummy file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires using InVesalius 3. The dataset is 'ct_cranium/0051'. The threshold maximum Hounsfield Unit (HU) must be exactly -100 or less to properly target Air/Sinuses. The generated 3D surface must be colored predominantly Blue (b > r and b > g). The final project must be saved exactly as '/home/ga/Documents/airway_study.inv3'.
